verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचवनं विविक्षु: पुत्रेषु भार्यां न्यस्य सहैव वा ।वन एव वसेच्छान्तस्तृतीयं भागमायुष: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācavanaṁ vivikṣuḥ putreṣubhāryāṁ nyasya sahaiva vāvana eva vasec chāntastṛtīyaṁ bhāgam āyuṣaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: One who desires to adopt the third order of life, vānaprastha, should enter the forest with a peaceful mind, leaving his wife with his mature sons, or else taking her along with him.

verses.sanskrit: कन्दमूलफलैर्वन्यैर्मेध्यैर्वृत्तिं प्रकल्पयेत् ।वसीत वल्कलं वासस्तृणपर्णाजिनानि वा ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: kanda-mūla-phalair vanyairmedhyair vṛttiṁ prakalpayetvasīta valkalaṁ vāsastṛṇa-parṇājināni vā
verses.text: Having adopted the vānaprastha order of life, one should arrange one’s sustenance by eating uncontaminated bulbs, roots and fruits that grow in the forest. One may dress oneself with tree bark, grass, leaves or animal skins.

verses.sanskrit: केशरोमनखश्मश्रुमलानि बिभृयाद् दत: ।न धावेदप्सु मज्जेत त्रिकालं स्थण्डिलेशय: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: keśa-roma-nakha-śmaśru-malāni bibhṛyād dataḥna dhāved apsu majjetatri kālaṁ sthaṇḍile-śayaḥ
verses.text: The vānaprastha should not groom the hair on his head, body or face, should not manicure his nails, should not pass stool and urine at irregular times and should not make a special endeavor for dental hygiene. He should be content to take bath in water three times daily and should sleep on the ground.

verses.sanskrit: ग्रीष्मे तप्येत पञ्चाग्नीन् वर्षास्वासारषाड्‍जले ।आकण्ठमग्न: शिशिर एवंवृत्तस्तपश्चरेत् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: grīṣme tapyeta pañcāgnīnvarṣāsv āsāra-ṣāḍ jaleākaṇtha-magnaḥ śiśiraevaṁ vṛttas tapaś caret
verses.text: Thus engaged as a vānaprastha, one should execute penance during the hottest summer days by subjecting oneself to burning fires on four sides and the blazing sun overhead; during the rainy season one should remain outside, subjecting oneself to torrents of rain; and in the freezing winter one should remain submerged in water up to one’s neck.

verses.sanskrit: अग्निपक्वं समश्न‍ीयात् कालपक्व‍मथापि वा ।उलूखलाश्मकुट्टो वा दन्तोलूखल एव वा ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: agni-pakvaṁ samaśnīyātkāla-pakvam athāpi vāulūkhalāśma-kuṭṭo vādantolūkhala eva vā
verses.text: One may eat foodstuffs prepared with fire, such as grains, or fruits ripened by time. One may grind one’s food with mortar and stone or with one’s own teeth.

verses.sanskrit: स्वयं सञ्चिनुयात् सर्वमात्मनो वृत्तिकारणम् ।देशकालबलाभिज्ञो नाददीतान्यदाहृतम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: svayaṁ sañcinuyāt sarvamātmano vṛtti-kāraṇamdeśa-kāla-balābhijñonādadītānyadāhṛtam
verses.text: The vānaprastha should personally collect whatever he requires for his bodily maintenance, carefully considering the time, place and his own capacity. He should never collect provisions for the future.

verses.sanskrit: वन्यैश्चरुपुरोडाशैर्निर्वपेत् कालचोदितान् ।न तु श्रौतेन पशुना मां यजेत वनाश्रमी ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: vanyaiś caru-puroḍāśairnirvapet kāla-coditānna tu śrautena paśunāmāṁ yajeta vanāśramī
verses.text: One who has accepted the vānaprastha order of life should perform seasonal sacrifices by offering oblations of caru and sacrificial cakes prepared from rice and other grains found in the forest. The vānaprastha, however, may never offer animal sacrifices to Me, even those sacrifices mentioned in the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: अग्निहोत्रं च दर्शश्च पौर्णमासश्च पूर्ववत् ।चातुर्मास्यानि च मुनेराम्नातानि च नैगमै: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: agnihotraṁ ca darśaś capaurṇamāsaś ca pūrva-vatcāturmāsyāni ca munerāmnātāni ca naigamaiḥ
verses.text: The vānaprastha should perform the agnihotra, darśa and paurṇamāsa sacrifices, as he did while in the gṛhastha-āśrama. He should also perform the vows and sacrifices of cāturmāsya, since all of these rituals are enjoined for the vānaprastha-āśrama by expert knowers of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: एवं चीर्णेन तपसा मुनिर्धमनिसन्तत: ।मां तपोमयमाराध्य ऋषिलोकादुपैति माम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ cīrṇena tapasāmunir dhamani-santataḥmāṁ tapo-mayam ārādhyaṛṣi-lokād upaiti mām
verses.text: The saintly vānaprastha, practicing severe penances and accepting only the bare necessities of life, becomes so emaciated that he appears to be mere skin and bones. Thus worshiping Me through severe penances, he goes to the Maharloka planet and then directly achieves Me.

verses.sanskrit: यस्त्वेतत् कृच्छ्रतश्चीर्णं तपो नि:श्रेयसं महत् ।कामायाल्पीयसे युञ्ज्याद् बालिश: कोऽपरस्तत: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: yas tv etat kṛcchrataś cīrṇaṁtapo niḥśreyasaṁ mahatkāmāyālpīyase yuñjyādbāliśaḥ ko ’paras tataḥ
verses.text: One who with long endeavor executes this painful but exalted penance, which awards ultimate liberation, simply to achieve insignificant sense gratification must be considered the greatest fool.

verses.sanskrit: यदासौ नियमेऽकल्पो जरया जातवेपथु: ।आत्मन्यग्नीन् समारोप्य मच्चित्तोऽग्निं समाविशेत् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: yadāsau niyame ’kalpojarayā jāta-vepathuḥātmany agnīn samāropyamac-citto ’gniṁ samāviśet
verses.text: If the vānaprastha is overtaken by old age and because of his trembling body is no longer able to execute his prescribed duties, he should place the sacrificial fire within his heart by meditation. Then, fixing his mind on Me, he should enter into the fire and give up his body.

verses.sanskrit: यदा कर्मविपाकेषु लोकेषु निरयात्मसु ।विरागो जायते सम्यङ् न्यस्ताग्नि: प्रव्रजेत्तत: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yadā karma-vipākeṣulokeṣu nirayātmasuvirāgo jāyate samyaṅnyastāgniḥ pravrajet tataḥ
verses.text: If the vānaprastha, understanding that even promotion to Brahmaloka is a miserable situation, develops complete detachment from all possible results of fruitive activities, then he may take the sannyāsa order of life.

verses.sanskrit: इष्ट्वा यथोपदेशं मां दत्त्वा सर्वस्वमृत्विजे ।अग्नीन् स्वप्राण आवेश्य निरपेक्ष: परिव्रजेत् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: iṣṭvā yathopadeśaṁ māṁdattvā sarva-svam ṛtvijeagnīn sva-prāṇa āveśyanirapekṣaḥ parivrajet
verses.text: Having worshiped Me according to scriptural injunctions and having given all one’s property to the sacrificial priest, one should place the fire sacrifice within oneself. Thus, with the mind completely detached, one should enter the sannyāsa order of life.

verses.sanskrit: विप्रस्य वै सन्न्यसतो देवा दारादिरूपिण: ।विघ्नान् कुर्वन्त्ययं ह्यस्मानाक्रम्य समियात् परम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: viprasya vai sannyasatodevā dārādi-rūpiṇaḥvighnān kurvanty ayaṁ hy asmānākramya samiyāt param
verses.text: “This man taking sannyāsa is going to surpass us and go back home, back to Godhead.” Thus thinking, the demigods create stumbling blocks on the path of the sannyāsī by appearing before him in the shape of his former wife or other women and attractive objects. But the sannyāsī should pay the demigods and their manifestations no heed.

verses.sanskrit: बिभृयाच्चेन्मुनिर्वास: कौपीनाच्छादनं परम् ।त्यक्तं न दण्डपात्राभ्यामन्यत् किञ्चिदनापदि ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: bibhṛyāc cen munir vāsaḥkaupīnācchādanaṁ paramtyaktaṁ na daṇḍa-pātrābhyāmanyat kiñcid anāpadi
verses.text: If the sannyāsī desires to wear something besides a mere kaupīna, he may use another cloth around his waist and hips to cover the kaupīna. Otherwise, if there is no emergency, he should not accept anything besides his daṇḍa and waterpot.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्टिपूतं न्यसेत् पादं वस्‍त्रपूतं पिबेज्जलम् ।सत्यपूतां वदेद् वाचं मन:पूतं समाचरेत् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭi-pūtaṁ nyaset pādaṁvastra-pūtaṁ pibej jalamsatya-pūtāṁ vaded vācaṁmanaḥ-pūtaṁ samācaret
verses.text: A saintly person should step or place his foot on the ground only after verifying with his eyes that there are no living creatures, such as insects, who might be injured by his foot. He should drink water only after filtering it through a portion of his cloth, and he should speak only words that possess the purity of truth. Similarly, he should perform only those activities his mind has carefully ascertained to be pure.

verses.sanskrit: मौनानीहानिलायामा दण्डा वाग्देहचेतसाम् ।न ह्येते यस्य सन्त्यङ्ग वेणुभिर्न भवेद् यति: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: maunānīhānilāyāmādaṇḍā vāg-deha-cetasāmna hy ete yasya santy aṅgaveṇubhir na bhaved yatiḥ
verses.text: One who has not accepted the three internal disciplines of avoiding useless speech, avoiding useless activities and controlling the life air can never be considered a sannyāsī merely because of his carrying bamboo rods.

verses.sanskrit: भिक्षां चतुर्षु वर्णेषु विगर्ह्यान् वर्जयंश्चरेत् ।सप्तागारानसङ्‍क्लृप्तांस्तुष्येल्ल‍ब्धेन तावता ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhikṣāṁ caturṣu varṇeṣuvigarhyān varjayaṁś caretsaptāgārān asaṅkḷptāṁstuṣyel labdhena tāvatā
verses.text: Rejecting those houses that are polluted and untouchable, one should approach without previous calculation seven houses and be satisfied with that which is obtained there by begging. According to necessity, one may approach each of the four occupational orders of society.

verses.sanskrit: बहिर्जलाशयं गत्वा तत्रोपस्पृश्य वाग्यत: ।विभज्य पावितं शेषं भुञ्जीताशेषमाहृतम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: bahir jalāśayaṁ gatvātatropaspṛśya vāg-yataḥvibhajya pāvitaṁ śeṣaṁbhuñjītāśeṣam āhṛtam
verses.text: Taking the food gathered through begging, one should leave the populated areas and go to a reservoir of water in a secluded place. There, having taken a bath and washed one’s hands thoroughly, one should distribute portions of the food to others who may request it. One should do this without speaking. Then, having thoroughly cleansed the remnants, one should eat everything on one’s plate, leaving nothing for future consumption.

verses.sanskrit: एकश्चरेन्महीमेतां नि:सङ्ग: संयतेन्द्रिय: ।आत्मक्रीड आत्मरत आत्मवान् समदर्शन: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: ekaś caren mahīm etāṁniḥsaṅgaḥ saṁyatendriyaḥātma-krīḍa ātma-rataātma-vān sama-darśanaḥ
verses.text: Without any material attachment, with senses fully controlled, remaining enthusiastic, and satisfied in realization of the Supreme Lord and his own self, the saintly person should travel about the earth alone. Having equal vision everywhere, he should be steady on the spiritual platform.

verses.sanskrit: विविक्तक्षेमशरणो मद्भ‍ावविमलाशय: ।आत्मानं चिन्तयेदेकमभेदेन मया मुनि: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: vivikta-kṣema-śaraṇomad-bhāva-vimalāśayaḥātmānaṁ cintayed ekamabhedena mayā muniḥ
verses.text: Dwelling in a safe and solitary place, his mind purified by constant thought of Me, the sage should concentrate on the soul alone, realizing it to be nondifferent from Me.

verses.sanskrit: अन्वीक्षेतात्मनो बन्धं मोक्षं च ज्ञाननिष्ठया ।बन्ध इन्द्रियविक्षेपो मोक्ष एषां च संयम: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: anvīkṣetātmano bandhaṁmokṣaṁ ca jñāna-niṣṭhayābandha indriya-vikṣepomokṣa eṣāṁ ca saṁyamaḥ
verses.text: By steady knowledge a sage should clearly ascertain the nature of the soul’s bondage and liberation. Bondage occurs when the senses are deviated to sense gratification, and complete control of the senses constitutes liberation.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान्नियम्य षड्‍वर्गं मद्भ‍ावेन चरेन्मुनि: ।विरक्त: क्षुद्रकामेभ्यो लब्ध्वात्मनि सुखं महत् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmān niyamya ṣaḍ-vargaṁmad-bhāvena caren muniḥviraktaḥ kṣudra-kāmebhyolabdhvātmani sukhaṁ mahat
verses.text: Therefore, completely controlling the five senses and the mind by Kṛṣṇa consciousness, a sage, having experienced spiritual bliss within the self, should live detached from insignificant material sense gratification.

verses.sanskrit: पुरग्रामव्रजान्सार्थान् भिक्षार्थं प्रविशंश्चरेत् ।पुण्यदेशसरिच्छैलवनाश्रमवतीं महीम् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: pura-grāma-vrajān sārthānbhikṣārthaṁ praviśaṁś caretpuṇya-deśa-saric-chaila-vanāśrama-vatīṁ mahīm
verses.text: The sage should travel in sanctified places, by flowing rivers and within the solitude of mountains and forests. He should enter the cities, towns and pasturing grounds and approach ordinary working men only to beg his bare sustenance.

verses.sanskrit: वानप्रस्थाश्रमपदेष्वभीक्ष्णं भैक्ष्यमाचरेत् ।संसिध्यत्याश्वसम्मोह: शुद्धसत्त्व: शिलान्धसा ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vānaprasthāśrama-padeṣvabhīkṣṇaṁ bhaikṣyam ācaretsaṁsidhyaty āśv asammohaḥśuddha-sattvaḥ śilāndhasā
verses.text: One in the vānaprastha order of life should always practice taking charity from others, for one is thereby freed from illusion and quickly becomes perfect in spiritual life. Indeed, one who subsists on food grains obtained in such a humble manner purifies his existence.

verses.sanskrit: नैतद् वस्तुतया पश्येद् द‍ृश्यमानं विनश्यति ।असक्तचित्तो विरमेदिहामुत्र चिकीर्षितात् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: naitad vastutayā paśyeddṛśyamānaṁ vinaśyatiasakta-citto viramedihāmutra-cikīrṣitāt
verses.text: One should never see as ultimate reality those material things which obviously will perish. With consciousness free from material attachment, one should retire from all activities meant for material progress in this life and the next.

verses.sanskrit: यदेतदात्मनि जगन्मनोवाक्प्राणसंहतम् ।सर्वं मायेति तर्केण स्वस्थस्त्यक्त्वा न तत् स्मरेत् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yad etad ātmani jaganmano-vāk-prāṇa-saṁhatamsarvaṁ māyeti tarkeṇasva-sthas tyaktvā na tat smaret
verses.text: One should logically consider the universe, which is situated within the Lord, and one’s own material body, which is composed of mind, speech and life air, to be ultimately products of the Lord’s illusory energy. Thus situated in the self, one should give up one’s faith in these things and should never again make them the object of one’s meditation.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञाननिष्ठो विरक्तो वा मद्भ‍क्तो वानपेक्षक: ।सलिङ्गानाश्रमांस्त्यक्त्वा चरेदविधिगोचर: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: jñāna-niṣṭho virakto vāmad-bhakto vānapekṣakaḥsa-liṅgān āśramāṁs tyaktvācared avidhi-gocaraḥ
verses.text: A learned transcendentalist dedicated to the cultivation of knowledge and thus detached from external objects, or My devotee who is detached even from desire for liberation — both neglect those duties based on external rituals or paraphernalia. Thus their conduct is beyond the range of rules and regulations.

verses.sanskrit: बुधो बालकवत् क्रीडेत् कुशलो जडवच्चरेत् ।वदेदुन्मत्तवद् विद्वान् गोचर्यां नैगमश्चरेत् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: budho bālaka-vat krīḍetkuśalo jaḍa-vac caretvaded unmatta-vad vidvāngo-caryāṁ naigamaś caret
verses.text: Although most wise, the paramahaṁsa should enjoy life like a child, oblivious to honor and dishonor; although most expert, he should behave like a stunted, incompetent person; although most learned, he should speak like an insane person; and although a scholar learned in Vedic regulations, he should behave in an unrestricted manner.

verses.sanskrit: वेदवादरतो न स्यान्न पाषण्डी न हैतुक: ।शुष्कवादविवादे न कञ्चित् पक्षं समाश्रयेत् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: veda-vāda-rato na syānna pāṣaṇḍī na haitukaḥśuṣka-vāda-vivāde nakañcit pakṣaṁ samāśrayet
verses.text: A devotee should never engage in the fruitive rituals mentioned in the karma-kāṇḍa section of the Vedas, nor should he become atheistic, acting or speaking in opposition to Vedic injunctions. Similarly, he should never speak like a mere logician or skeptic or take any side whatsoever in useless arguments.

verses.sanskrit: नोद्विजेत जनाद् धीरो जनं चोद्वेजयेन्न तु ।अतिवादांस्तितिक्षेत नावमन्येत कञ्चन ।देहमुद्दिश्य पशुवद् वैरं कुर्यान्न केनचित् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: nodvijeta janād dhīrojanaṁ codvejayen na tuati-vādāṁs titikṣetanāvamanyeta kañcanadeham uddiśya paśu-vadvairaṁ kuryān na kenacit
verses.text: A saintly person should never let others frighten or disturb him and, similarly, should never frighten or disturb other people. He should tolerate the insults of others and should never himself belittle anyone. He should never create hostility with anyone for the sake of the material body, for he would thus be no better than an animal.

verses.sanskrit: एक एव परो ह्यात्मा भूतेष्वात्मन्यवस्थित: ।यथेन्दुरुदपात्रेषु भूतान्येकात्मकानि च ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: eka eva paro hy ātmābhūteṣv ātmany avasthitaḥyathendur uda-pātreṣubhūtāny ekātmakāni ca
verses.text: The one Supreme Lord is situated within all material bodies and within everyone’s soul. Just as the moon is reflected in innumerable reservoirs of water, the Supreme Lord, although one, is present within everyone. Thus every material body is ultimately composed of the energy of the one Supreme Lord.

verses.sanskrit: अलब्ध्वा न विषीदेत काले कालेऽशनं क्व‍‍चित् ।लब्ध्वा न हृष्येद् धृतिमानुभयं दैवतन्त्रितम् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: alabdhvā na viṣīdetakāle kāle ’śanaṁ kvacitlabdhvā na hṛṣyed dhṛtimānubhayaṁ daiva-tantritam
verses.text: If at times one does not obtain proper food one should not be depressed, and when one obtains sumptuous food one should not rejoice. Being fixed in determination, one should understand both situations to be under the control of God.

verses.sanskrit: आहारार्थं समीहेत युक्तं तत् प्राणधारणम् ।तत्त्वं विमृश्यते तेन तद् विज्ञाय विमुच्यते ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: āhārārthaṁ samīhetayuktaṁ tat-prāṇa-dhāraṇamtattvaṁ vimṛśyate tenatad vijñāya vimucyate
verses.text: If required, one should endeavor to get sufficient foodstuffs, because it is always necessary and proper to maintain one’s health. When the senses, mind and life air are fit, one can contemplate spiritual truth, and by understanding the truth one is liberated.

verses.sanskrit: यद‍ृच्छयोपपन्नान्नमद्याच्छ्रेष्ठमुतापरम् ।तथा वासस्तथा शय्यां प्राप्तं प्राप्तं भजेन्मुनि: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yadṛcchayopapannānnamadyāc chreṣṭham utāparamtathā vāsas tathā śayyāṁprāptaṁ prāptaṁ bhajen muniḥ
verses.text: A sage should accept the food, clothing and bedding — be they of excellent or inferior quality — that come of their own accord.

verses.sanskrit: शौचमाचमनं स्‍नानं न तु चोदनया चरेत् ।अन्यांश्च नियमाञ्ज्ञानी यथाहं लीलयेश्वर: ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śaucam ācamanaṁ snānaṁna tu codanayā caretanyāṁś ca niyamāñ jñānīyathāhaṁ līlayeśvaraḥ
verses.text: Just as I, the Supreme Lord, execute regulative duties by My own free will, similarly, one who has realized knowledge of Me should maintain general cleanliness, purify his hands with water, take bath and execute other regulative duties not by force but by his own free will.

verses.sanskrit: न हि तस्य विकल्पाख्या या च मद्वीक्षया हता ।आदेहान्तात् क्व‍‍चित् ख्यातिस्तत: सम्पद्यते मया ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hi tasya vikalpākhyāyā ca mad-vīkṣayā hatāā-dehāntāt kvacit khyātistataḥ sampadyate mayā
verses.text: A realized soul no longer sees anything as separate from Me, for his realized knowledge of Me has destroyed such illusory perception. Since the material body and mind were previously accustomed to this kind of perception, it may sometimes appear to recur; but at the time of death the self-realized soul achieves opulences equal to Mine.

verses.sanskrit: दु:खोदर्केषु कामेषु जातनिर्वेद आत्मवान् ।अजिज्ञासितमद्धर्मो मुनिं गुरुमुपव्रजेत् ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: duḥkhodarkeṣu kāmeṣujāta-nirveda ātmavānajijñāsita-mad-dharmomuniṁ gurum upavrajet
verses.text: One who is detached from sense gratification, knowing its result to be miserable, and who desires spiritual perfection, but who has not seriously analyzed the process for obtaining Me, should approach a bona fide and learned spiritual master.

verses.sanskrit: तावत् परिचरेद् भक्त: श्रद्धावाननसूयक: ।यावद् ब्रह्म विजानीयान्मामेव गुरुमाद‍ृत: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāvat paricared bhaktaḥśraddhāvān anasūyakaḥyāvad brahma vijānīyānmām eva gurum ādṛtaḥ
verses.text: Until a devotee has clearly realized spiritual knowledge, he should continue with great faith and respect and without envy to render personal service to the guru, who is nondifferent from Me.

verses.sanskrit: यस्त्वसंयतषड्‍वर्ग: प्रचण्डेन्द्रियसारथि: ।ज्ञानवैराग्यरहितस्‍त्रिदण्डमुपजीवति ॥ ४० ॥सुरानात्मानमात्मस्थं निह्नुते मां च धर्महा ।अविपक्व‍कषायोऽस्मादमुष्माच्च विहीयते ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: yas tv asaṁyata-ṣaḍ-vargaḥpracaṇḍendriya-sārathiḥjñāna-vairāgya-rahitastridaṇḍam upajīvati
verses.text: One who has not controlled the six forms of illusion [lust, anger, greed, excitement, false pride and intoxication], whose intelligence, the leader of the senses, is extremely attached to material things, who is bereft of knowledge and detachment, who adopts the sannyāsa order of life to make a living, who denies the worshipable demigods, his own self and the Supreme Lord within himself, thus ruining all religious principles, and who is still infected by material contamination, is deviated and lost both in this life and the next.

verses.sanskrit: भिक्षोर्धर्म: शमोऽहिंसा तप ईक्षा वनौकस: ।गृहिणो भूतरक्षेज्या द्विजस्याचार्यसेवनम् ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhikṣor dharmaḥ śamo ’hiṁsātapa īkṣā vanaukasaḥgṛhiṇo bhūta-rakṣejyādvijasyācārya-sevanam
verses.text: The main religious duties of a sannyāsī are equanimity and nonviolence, whereas for the vānaprastha austerity and philosophical understanding of the difference between the body and soul are prominent. The main duties of a householder are to give shelter to all living entities and perform sacrifices, and the brahmacārī is mainly engaged in serving the spiritual master.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मचर्यं तप: शौचं सन्तोषो भूतसौहृदम् ।गृहस्थस्याप्यृतौ गन्तु: सर्वेषां मदुपासनम् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: brahmacaryaṁ tapaḥ śaucaṁsantoṣo bhūta-sauhṛdamgṛhasthasyāpy ṛtau gantuḥsarveṣāṁ mad-upāsanam
verses.text: A householder may approach his wife for sex only at the time prescribed for begetting children. Otherwise, the householder should practice celibacy, austerity, cleanliness of mind and body, satisfaction in his natural position, and friendship toward all living entities. Worship of Me is to be practiced by all human beings, regardless of social or occupational divisions.

verses.sanskrit: इति मां य: स्वधर्मेण भजेन् नित्यमनन्यभाक् ।सर्वभूतेषु मद्भ‍ावो मद्भ‍‍क्तिं विन्दते द‍ृढाम् ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti māṁ yaḥ sva-dharmeṇabhajen nityam ananya-bhāksarva-bhūteṣu mad-bhāvomad-bhaktiṁ vindate dṛḍhām
verses.text: One who worships Me by his prescribed duty, having no other object of worship, and who remains conscious of Me as present in all living entities, achieves unflinching devotional service unto Me.

verses.sanskrit: भक्त्योद्धवानपायिन्या सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् ।सर्वोत्पत्त्यप्ययं ब्रह्म कारणं मोपयाति स: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhaktyoddhavānapāyinyāsarva-loka-maheśvaramsarvotpatty-apyayaṁ brahmakāraṇaṁ mopayāti saḥ
verses.text: My dear Uddhava, I am the Supreme Lord of all worlds, and I create and destroy this universe, being its ultimate cause. I am thus the Absolute Truth, and one who worships Me with unfailing devotional service comes to Me.

verses.sanskrit: इति स्वधर्मनिर्णिक्तसत्त्वो निर्ज्ञातमद्गति: ।ज्ञानविज्ञानसम्पन्नो नचिरात् समुपैति माम् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti sva-dharma-nirṇikta-sattvo nirjñāta-mad-gatiḥjñāna-vijñāna-sampannona cirāt samupaiti mām
verses.text: Thus, one who has purified his existence by execution of his prescribed duties, who fully understands My supreme position and who is endowed with scriptural and realized knowledge, very soon achieves Me.

verses.sanskrit: वर्णाश्रमवतां धर्म एष आचारलक्षण: ।स एव मद्भ‍‍क्तियुतो नि:श्रेयसकर: पर: ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: varṇāśramavatāṁ dharmaeṣa ācāra-lakṣaṇaḥsa eva mad-bhakti-yutoniḥśreyasa-karaḥ paraḥ
verses.text: Those who are followers of this varṇāśrama system accept religious principles according to authorized traditions of proper conduct. When such varṇāśrama duties are dedicated to Me in loving service, they award the supreme perfection of life.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्तेऽभिहितं साधो भवान् पृच्छति यच्च माम् ।यथा स्वधर्मसंयुक्तो भक्तो मां समियात् परम् ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: etat te ’bhihitaṁ sādhobhavān pṛcchati yac ca māmyathā sva-dharma-saṁyuktobhakto māṁ samiyāt param
verses.text: My dear saintly Uddhava, I have now described to you, just as you inquired, the means by which My devotee, perfectly engaged in his prescribed duty, can come back to Me, the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
